system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight section
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (5 9 8 4 6 10 11 7)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1a: Transition from bend to outlet duct
    hex (6 12 11 7 3 14 15 10)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 1 0)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (19 18 17 16)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (1 10 9 5)
            (10 13 12 9)
            (13 17 16 12)
            (19 17 16 12)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 3 0)
            (2 6 7 3)
            (6 10 11 7)
            (10 14 15 11)
            (14 18 19 15)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 4 0)
            (7 11 8 4)
            (12 15 11 7)
            (12 16 19 15)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (5 6 2 1)
            (9 10 6 5)
            (13 15 14 10)
            (17 19 18 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS

    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;

thermoType

    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.96;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1004.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.8e-05;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='k', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

    }

        value           uniform 1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block hex (6 12 11 7 3 14 15 10) has inward-pointing faces

The error is very specific:
```
Block hex (6 12 11 7 3 14 15 10) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(6 3 10 7) 4(7 10 15 11) 4(3 14 15 10)
```

This means Block 1a (which is the third block in the `blocks` list, defined as `hex (6 12 11 7 3 14 15 10)`) has one or more faces whose normals point inward rather than outward. The issue is specifically with the face formed by vertices (6, 12, 11, 7).

Let me examine the vertices involved:
- Vertex 6: (0, -25, -25)
- Vertex 12: (25, -75, 25)
- Vertex 11: (25, -50, 25)
- Vertex 7: (0, -25, 25)

The face (6 12 11 7) connects these four vertices. To determine if it's inward-pointing, we need to see if the normal follows the right-hand rule correctly. The problem is that this hex block represents the transition piece connecting the bend to the outlet duct, and the vertex ordering creates a non-convex or inverted hexahedron.

### Root Cause

The fundamental issue across all attempts is that the **vertex numbering scheme doesn't produce valid convex hexahedra**. The blocks don't share internal faces consistently, and some blocks have faces that are geometrically problematic (coplanar edges, incorrect adjacency).

### Proposed Solution: Redesign the block topology

Instead of trying to fix individual block definitions, redesign the entire mesh to ensure all blocks are proper convex hexahedra with consistent shared face windings. Here's a recommended approach:

**Redefine the blocks with proper vertex ordering and ensure adjacent blocks share faces with matching (opposite) vertex orderings:**

1. **Block 0 (Inlet straight section)**: `hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4)` — Keep as-is (already fixed in Attempt 2)
2. **Block 1 (Upper bend section)**: `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11)` — reorder to get proper winding
3. **Block 1a (Lower bend/transition)**: `hex (7 10 14 11 6 3 15 12)` — reorder appropriately
4. **Block 2 (Outdoor straight section)**: `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)` — verify winding

But the real problem is the **geometry itself**. The vertices create blocks where shared faces don't align properly. A better approach would be to restructure the mesh into fewer, well-formed blocks. For example, split the domain into just 3 blocks:

- **Block A (Inlet duct, x=-50 to x=0)**: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` with proper winding
- **Block B (Bend section, x=0 to x=25, y=-25 to y=0)**: Split into two blocks:
  - Upper half: `hex (4 7 11 8 5 0 10 9)` — but vertex 0/4 are at same x, need careful alignment
- **Block C (Outlet duct, x=25 to x=-500, y=-75 to y=-25)**: `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)`

However, given the complexity and the fact that previous attempts have repeatedly hit winding/ordering issues, the most robust solution is:

### Recommendation: Adopt the exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial and only adjust vertex coordinates

Since the user requirement says "Follow the same geometry as the tutorial," take the **exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial** (which is known to work), and only replace the vertex coordinate values to match the user's specified positions:

- Replace the vertex coordinates in the tutorial's `blockMeshDict` with the user's vertices: (-50, 25, 25), (-50, 25, -25), ..., (-500, -75, 25), (-500, -75, -25)
- Keep all block definitions, edge definitions, boundary definitions, and the overall topology identical to the working tutorial version
- Only the numeric vertex positions change

This avoids all the winding/ordering issues entirely, since the tutorial's block topology is proven to be valid. After copying the tutorial's block structure with the user's vertex coordinates, update the boundary condition names in the `0/` files to match whatever patch names the tutorial uses (typically `inlet`, `outlet`, `walls`).

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

Cascading from blockMesh failure. Will resolve once blockMesh succeeds.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
